ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07820Larry Mitchell papers7820Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

humsxThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:02 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Larry Mitchell papers
Mitchell, Larry.
7820
6 cubic feet. 6 cubic feet.
1954-2005. Collection material in English Mitchell's publications, drafts of his and other people's writings (including a draft of a Harvey Fierstein piece), interviews, reviews and awards for his writing and the work of Calamus Books, and personal and family papers, including journals and diaries. Diaries, publications. 9128155
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
American author and publisher, born in Muncie, Indiana in 1938. Mitchell was the founder of Calamus Books, an early small press devoted to gay male literature, and a cofounder of Gay Presses of New York in 1981. Mitchell also wrote fiction dealing with the gay male experience in New York City during the 1970s and 1980s, including the book of short stories My life as a mole (1989) and the novel The terminal bar (1982). The feature film Acid snow (1998) is based on Mitchell's novel of the same name. He died in Ithaca, New York in 2012.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Mitchell's publications, drafts of his and other people's writings (including a draft of a Harvey Fierstein piece), video recording of Marsha P. Johnson, an African American drag queen who was a prominent participant in the 1969 Stonewall uprising in New York City and was an outspoken advocate for gay rights. Also includes interviews, reviews and awards for his writing (including the Lambda Literary Award) and the work of Calamus Books, as well as personal and family papers, including journals and diaries and a copy of Mitchell's baby book.
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
January 2016
EAD encoding:
Theo Wolf, January 2016
Date modified:
Jude Corina, January 2016
Restrictions on Use:
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: TR-10647-8.
Cite As:
Larry Mitchell Papers, #7820. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Publications and Books- cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

Manuscripts for publication. Gay men -- New York (State) -- New York -- Fiction. Gays\' writings, American. Publishers and publishing -- United States. Johnson, Marsha. Mitchell, Larry.
